name: apple fennel salad
instructions: finely dice the apple. mix with either fennel or celery (or both). dress with lemon (juice + zest) and olive oil — that's the entire vinaigrette. red onion is optional; if used, sautee or pickle it well with vinegar first. slivered almonds if you have them around. finely chiffonade fresh mint leaves (off stems, fine cut — see herbs and greens article) and fold in just before serving.
notes:
VINAIGRETTE IS JUST LEMON: no vinegar needed. lemon is acidic enough on its own — unlike tangerine, which is too sweet to carry a salad acid by itself.

WHY TANGERINE MINT VINAIGRETTE CLASHES: it's too sweet for this salad even with vinegar added. apple is already bringing sweetness; you need a sharp acid here, not another sweet-acid layer.

RED ONION: only works if properly pickled or sauteed first — raw red onion is too aggressive against the apple/fennel/lemon balance.

CHIFFONADED MINT: chiffonaded fresh mint works beautifully here. Mint pairs with the apple's sweetness and the lemon's tang — both natural partners. Fold in just before serving so the leaves stay fresh. (Note this doesn't contradict the tangerine-mint-vinaigrette clash above: the issue is tangerine's sweetness compounding with the apple, not the mint itself. Mint alone is welcome; mint-as-a-vehicle-for-tangerine is not.)
